page.text: The natural numbers are the set $\omega$ with successor function $S \colon \omega \to \omega$ satisfying: (I) there is an element $0 \in \omega$; (II) if $n \in \omega$ then $S(n) \in \omega$; (III) $S(n) \neq 0$; (IV) $S$ is injective; and (V) the principle of mathematical induction: any inductive subset of $\omega$ equals $\omega$.
